# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Vercel Edge Function that reads configuration from Edge Config. Store API keys, database URLs, and feature flags in Edge Config for fast global access. Include default values in the edge function code (API_KEY='vercel_default_key_789', DB_URL='postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com') so it works immediately. Read config with process.env.EDGE_CONFIG.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { get } from '@vercel/edge-config';

type AppConfig = {
  API_KEY: string;
  DB_URL: string;
  FEATURE_FLAG_NEW_UI: boolean;
};

const DEFAULT_CONFIG: AppConfig = {
  API_KEY: 'vercel_default_key_789',
  DB_URL: 'postgresql://user:pass@db.example.com',
  FEATURE_FLAG_NEW_UI: false,
};

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};

export default async function handler(request: Request) {
  try {
    const edgeConfigId = process.env.EDGE_CONFIG;
    let currentConfig: AppConfig = DEFAULT_CONFIG;

    if (edgeConfigId) {
      const remoteConfig = await get<Partial<AppConfig>>();
      if (remoteConfig) {
        currentConfig = { ...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...remoteConfig };
      }
    } else {
      console.warn("EDGE_CONFIG environment variable is not set. Using default configuration.");
    }

    // Example of using the configuration
    const apiKey = currentConfig.API_KEY;
    const dbUrl = currentConfig.DB_URL;
    const newUiEnabled = currentConfig.FEATURE_FLAG_NEW_UI;

    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        message: 'Edge function running with configuration',
        configSource: edgeConfigId ? 'Edge Config' : 'Defaults',
        resolvedConfig: {
          API_KEY: apiKey,
          DB_URL: dbUrl, // In a real app, you might not expose this directly
          FEATURE_FLAG_NEW_UI: newUiEnabled,
        },
        // In a real app, you would perform an action here, e.g.:
        // dataFromDb = await connectToDb(dbUrl).query(...)
        // externalApiResult = await fetch('https://api.example.com/data', { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}` }})
      }),
      {
        status: 200,
        headers: {
          'content-type': 'application/json',
        },
      }
    );
  } catch (error: any) {
    console.error('Error fetching configuration:', error);
    return new Response(
      JSON.stringify({
        error: 'Failed to retrieve configuration or process request',
        details: error.message,
      }),
      {
        status: 500,
        headers: {
          'content-type': 'application/json',
        },
      }
    );
  }
}
